definition: Compare community-composition samples using Bray-Curtis similarity, UniFrac-derived similarity, generalized UniFrac, or another beta-diversity distance. This audits whether model performance depends on overlap in taxonomic abundance, community membership, or phylogenetic composition.
example_usage: python composition_braycurtis_similarity.py --train train_microbiome.csv --eval eval_microbiome.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --feature-cols-prefix taxon_

citations.title: Associating microbiome composition with environmental covariates using generalized UniFrac distances

evidence.summary: The paper motivates distance-based analysis of microbiome composition and generalized UniFrac distances.
It defines UniFrac distances between microbial communities using phylogenetic branch length and abundance.
It concludes that generalized UniFrac detects a wider range of biologically relevant community changes.

limitations: Bray-Curtis ignores phylogeny; UniFrac requires a valid phylogenetic tree and consistent taxonomic preprocessing.
Sequencing depth, rarefaction, compositionality, and batch effects can alter beta-diversity distances.
Taxonomic overlap may not capture functional or strain-level novelty.
quality_gates: Report abundance transform, filtering, normalization, and beta-diversity metric.
Use UniFrac or generalized UniFrac when phylogenetic relationships are scientifically important.
Audit batch, study, geography, and host covariates separately from taxonomic similarity.
